dependencies: Recovered fiber (OCC) as primary raw material for recycled containerboard
Virgin fiber from hardwoods and softwoods for virgin containerboard
Energy intensive production processes dependent on natural gas, oil, electricity and biomass fuel
Transportation services primarily by truck, rail and sea
risks: Price volatility in recovered fiber (OCC) markets
Demand from Asian purchasers creating OCC supply/demand imbalances
Wood fiber price increases due to government subsidies for energy generation
Energy price fluctuations affecting production costs
Reduced availability of trucks, rail cars or cargo ships due to labor shortages
Climate and weather conditions affecting raw material availability
Supply chain disruptions from geopolitical events

regulatory_risks: Environmental laws and regulations requiring compliance costs
Greenhouse gas emissions trading schemes (EU Emission Trading Scheme, Quebec cap-and-trade, Washington Climate Commitment Act)
Climate-related regulations and carbon tax mandates
Fuel efficiency standards increasing transportation costs
Occupational health and safety laws and regulations
Privacy, security and data protection laws (GDPR and evolving U.S. privacy laws)
Trade policy changes including tariff and customs regulations
Bribery and anti-corruption laws (U.S. Foreign Corrupt Practices Act, UK Bribery Act, French Sapin II Law, Irish Criminal Justice Act)
Income tax law changes in multiple jurisdictions
Pillar Two minimum effective tax rate of 15% for companies with revenue above €750 million
Environmental remediation requirements at owned and formerly owned facilities
Permit requirements and potential revocation or modification
Laws banning single-use paper bags or charging fees
Packaging waste and circular economy regulations

sanction_risks: Sanctions compliance requirements across multiple jurisdictions
Risk of trading with sanctioned countries, individuals or organizations
Impact of geopolitical conflicts such as Russia-Ukraine war
Expropriation risk (Venezuela operations seized by government)
Currency devaluation and hyperinflation in Argentina
